dict.string: Offers weather lookup or update check, then shows current weather data as notification, result, and HTML card.

Shortcuts generated by Shortcuts Playground.
Uses the free Open-Meteo endpoints for geocoding and current weather data. No API key is required.
The Open-Meteo forecast request uses celsius or fahrenheit based on the country code of the found city. Fahrenheit is used for US, BS, KY, LR, and MM; all other countries use celsius.
ALLOW_MANUAL_UNIT_CONVERSION: Open-Meteo performs the celsius/fahrenheit conversion through the temperature_unit request parameter; Shortcuts only displays the returned value with the matching unit symbol.
The update check reads the latest public GitHub release from Schrotty74/Shortcuts and opens the release page when a newer version is available. The shortcut cannot silently replace itself; Apple requires user confirmation during import.
Lets the user choose weather lookup or a GitHub release update check. - Get Weather continues with the city prompt below. - Check for Update reads the latest public GitHub release.
Chooses visible shortcut language from device settings. - German is used only when the localized weekday is German and the time zone is in AT, DE, CH, or LI. - All other devices use English.
Sets all visible text variables for this run. - German branch is only for DACH/LI German device settings. - English branch is the fallback for every other device.
Checks whether the device time zone is in the DACH/LI area. - Input uses the time-zone match count above. - A match keeps the German text branch active.
Für welche Stadt möchtest du das Wetter abrufen?
Bitte prüfe den Stadtnamen und versuche es erneut.
Der neueste GitHub-Release wird jetzt geöffnet. Importiere dort den neuen Kurzbefehl, um zu aktualisieren.
Installierte Version v1.2.3 ist der neueste GitHub-Release.

{"0":"Klarer Himmel","1":"Überwiegend klar","2":"Teilweise bewölkt","3":"Bedeckt","45":"Nebel","48":"Nebel mit Reifablagerung","51":"Leichter Nieselregen","53":"Mäßiger Nieselregen","55":"Starker Nieselregen","56":"Leichter gefrierender Nieselregen","57":"Starker gefrierender Nieselregen","61":"Leichter Regen","63":"Mäßiger Regen","65":"Starker Regen","66":"Leichter gefrierender Regen","67":"Starker gefrierender Regen","71":"Leichter Schneefall","73":"Mäßiger Schneefall","75":"Starker Schneefall","77":"Schneegriesel","80":"Leichte Regenschauer","81":"Mäßige Regenschauer","82":"Starke Regenschauer","85":"Leichte Schneeschauer","86":"Starke Schneeschauer","95":"Gewitter","96":"Gewitter mit leichtem Hagel","99":"Gewitter mit starkem Hagel"}
What do you want to do?
Which city would you like weather for?
Please check the city name and try again.
The latest GitHub release will open now. Import the new shortcut there to update.
Weather for City is up to date
Installed version v1.2.3 is the latest GitHub release.

{"0":"Clear sky","1":"Mainly clear","2":"Partly cloudy","3":"Overcast","45":"Fog","48":"Depositing rime fog","51":"Light drizzle","53":"Moderate drizzle","55":"Dense drizzle","56":"Light freezing drizzle","57":"Dense freezing drizzle","61":"Slight rain","63":"Moderate rain","65":"Heavy rain","66":"Light freezing rain","67":"Heavy freezing rain","71":"Slight snow fall","73":"Moderate snow fall","75":"Heavy snow fall","77":"Snow grains","80":"Slight rain showers","81":"Moderate rain showers","82":"Violent rain showers","85":"Slight snow showers","86":"Heavy snow showers","95":"Thunderstorm","96":"Thunderstorm with slight hail","99":"Thunderstorm with heavy hail"}
What do you want to do?
Which city would you like weather for?
Please check the city name and try again.
The latest GitHub release will open now. Import the new shortcut there to update.
Weather for City is up to date
Installed version v1.2.3 is the latest GitHub release.

{"0":"Clear sky","1":"Mainly clear","2":"Partly cloudy","3":"Overcast","45":"Fog","48":"Depositing rime fog","51":"Light drizzle","53":"Moderate drizzle","55":"Dense drizzle","56":"Light freezing drizzle","57":"Dense freezing drizzle","61":"Slight rain","63":"Moderate rain","65":"Heavy rain","66":"Light freezing rain","67":"Heavy freezing rain","71":"Slight snow fall","73":"Moderate snow fall","75":"Heavy snow fall","77":"Snow grains","80":"Slight rain showers","81":"Moderate rain showers","82":"Violent rain showers","85":"Slight snow showers","86":"Heavy snow showers","95":"Thunderstorm","96":"Thunderstorm with slight hail","99":"Thunderstorm with heavy hail"}
Branches on the selected start action. - First list item runs the weather lookup. - Second list item checks GitHub for updates.

Uses a saved city when available, or asks for a new city and saves it. - Saved city stays inside this shortcut via Stored Content. - New city input is normalized before Open-Meteo search. - The geocoding request loads multiple results for ambiguous city names.
Chooses whether a new location search is needed. - Change City asks for a new city and saves the selected location later. - Get Weather reuses a saved selected location when available.
Branches on whether a saved selected location exists. - If present, it becomes Selected Location immediately. - If missing, the shortcut asks for a city once and searches Open-Meteo.

Runs Open-Meteo geocoding only when a saved selected location is not available. - Needs Geocoding is set by the saved-location block above. - A saved selected location skips this whole search and chooser block.
Bricht mit einer verständlichen Meldung ab, wenn die Suche keinen Treffer liefert. - Input verwendet die Suchergebnisse aus Get Dictionary Value.
Selects the correct geocoding result. - If only one result exists, it is used directly. - If multiple results exist, the user chooses a location such as Columbia — South Carolina, United States.
Branches on ambiguous location results. - Input uses the result count above. - More than one result opens a visible location picker. - One result continues directly.
Builds the visible location choices. - Repeat Item is one Open-Meteo location result. - Each row uses city, admin area, and country. - Repeat Results becomes the list shown to the user.
Finds the selected location result. - The visible row is rebuilt for each Open-Meteo result. - When the row matches the chosen item, that Repeat Item becomes Selected Location.
Checks whether this rebuilt row is the chosen location. - Input uses the Location Choice text above. - Matching row sets Selected Location to the current Repeat Item.
Reads coordinates and display values from the selected Open-Meteo location result.
Saves the selected location only after a new Open-Meteo search. - Reusing a saved location does not write the file again. - This keeps repeated Get Weather runs quieter.

Saves the selected Open-Meteo location for future runs. - The saved file contains only city name, country code, latitude, and longitude. - Get Weather can use this saved location without showing the location chooser again.

Chooses celsius or fahrenheit from the selected city country code. - Country Code comes from the selected geocoding result above. - Match Text checks US, BS, KY, LR, and MM. - If the match count is greater than 0, Fahrenheit is used. - Otherwise celsius is used.
Branches on whether the country code matched a Fahrenheit country. - Input uses the match count from Count above. - Greater than 0 means Fahrenheit. - Otherwise celsius is used.
Shows a larger text result and then a styled HTML weather card.

Current Weather
￼
￼ ￼
Weather
TodayToday
SceneWeather
Temperature scale: -15 °C to +50 °C
Checks the latest public GitHub release. - Current installed version is v1.2.3. - If GitHub reports a newer tag, the release page is opened so the user can import it.
Compares the installed version with the latest GitHub release. - Input uses Latest Version Text from the GitHub response. - If it is not v1.2.3, an update is available.
